 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","title":"EPIC: CI/CD & Quality Gates","description":"# Epic: CI/CD & Quality Gates

## Overview
Set up comprehensive CI/CD pipeline to ensure quality and enable confident deployments.

## Background & Reasoning
A project of this scope needs automated quality gates:
- Catch bugs before they reach users
- Verify installer actually works on real Ubuntu
- Ensure website builds correctly
- Maintain code quality standards

## CI Components

### 1. Code Quality
- ESLint for TypeScript/JavaScript
- Prettier for formatting
- shellcheck for bash scripts
- TypeScript type checking

### 2. Website Tests
- Unit tests for components
- Build verification
- Lighthouse CI for performance

### 3. Installer Tests
- Spin Ubuntu VM (GitHub Actions runner or cloud)
- Run full install.sh
- Execute acfs doctor
- Verify all tools present

### 4. Integration Tests
- Manifest parsing
- Generator outputs
- Doctor check coverage

## GitHub Actions Workflows

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
- lint-and-type-check
- test-website
- test-installer-ubuntu-25
- shellcheck-scripts
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All PRs require passing CI
- [ ] Installer tested on real Ubuntu
- [ ] Website build verified
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all .sh files
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] Deployment preview for PRs

## Considerations
- Keep CI fast (< 10 min for most checks)
- Installer test can be slower (separate workflow)
- Use caching aggressively
- Matrix test multiple Ubuntu versions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:08.720517-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:47:02.195651-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:47:02.195651-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["ci","epic"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for website CI","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Website CI

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow for website quality gates.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/website.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. lint-and-typecheck
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run lint
- run: bun run type-check
```

### 2. build
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run build
```

### 3. test (if tests exist)
```yaml
- run: bun test
```

### 4. lighthouse (optional)
```yaml
- uses: treosh/lighthouse-ci-action@v10
  with:
    urls: |
      http://localhost:3000/
    uploadArtifacts: true
```

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests to main

## Caching
```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v3
  with:
    path: ~/.bun/install/cache
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs on every PR
- [ ] Lint + typecheck required to pass
- [ ] Build verification
- [ ] Caching for speed
- [ ] Clear failure messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:08.308979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:48:52.669536-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:48:52.669536-05:00","close_reason":"Created GitHub Actions workflow for website CI. Fixed barrel import issues causing SSG build failures by switching to direct component imports. All lint, type-check, and build steps now pass.","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:08.310651-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for installer testing","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Installer Testing

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow that tests the installer on real Ubuntu.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/installer.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. shellcheck
Fast job that runs on every PR:
```yaml
- uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
  with:
    scandir: './scripts'
    additional_files: 'install.sh'
```

### 2. test-ubuntu-25
Full installer test (may be slow):
```yaml
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
container:
  image: ubuntu:25.04
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4
  - name: Run installer
    run: |
      bash install.sh --yes --mode vibe
  - name: Run doctor
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "acfs doctor"
  - name: Verify key tools
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "ntm --help"
      su - ubuntu -c "ubs --help"
      # ... etc
```

### 3. test-ubuntu-24
Same but on 24.04 LTS for compatibility.

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests touching install.sh or scripts/

## Matrix Testing
```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    ubuntu: ['24.04', '25.04']
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all scripts
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 25.04
- [ ] All tools verified after install
- [ ] acfs doctor exits 0","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.687302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:46:57.410761-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:46:57.410761-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.688994-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -45,7 +45,7 @@
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ixt","title":"Fix set -e crash in onboard status progress bar","description":"`onboard status` used `seq 1 0` in command substitution under `set -euo pipefail`, which can cause the script to exit when progress is 0. Replace with safe loops; also make common counter increments `+= 1` so scripts remain safe when sourced under set -e.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:04:33.527609-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:07:15.994218-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:07:15.994218-05:00","close_reason":"Fix onboard status progress bar set -e crash (seq 1 0) by using safe loops; also swap remaining ++ counters to += 1 for set -e safety.","labels":["onboard","reliability"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwa","title":"Fail-fast remote curl|bash invocations in install.sh","description":"Several remote installer invocations in install.sh run via subshells without pipefail (e.g., oh-my-zsh uses sh -c with curl output, and multiple run_as_target bash -c "curl ... | sh"). If curl fails, the subshell can still return success and the installer continues. Fix by running these under bash with set -euo pipefail and passing args safely, so download/exec failures abort correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:17:16.089137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","close_reason":"Wrap remote installer subshells with set -euo pipefail so curl failures abort; also make stack remote installer helper pass args safely and avoid masking download errors.","labels":["installer","reliability"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette

## Description
Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding.

## Background
The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy.

## Implementation

### Vendor Location
`acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Sync Script
`scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Sync NTM command palette from upstream

SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md"
DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md"

curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST"
echo "Synced command_palette.md"
```

### Install Location
Deployed to: `~/.acfs/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Onboard Integration
In Mission 6, show the palette using:
- bat (pretty) if available
- less as fallback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sync script works
- [ ] Palette included in installer
- [ ] Displayed in onboard Mission 6
- [ ] Readable over SSH","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","close_reason":"Vendored NTM command_palette.md to acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/. Added sync script scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh with --check mode. Commit 2a09af8.","labels":["onboard","task"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lbo","title":"doctor: include user tool dirs in PATH","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh relies on PATH-only lookups, so running `acfs doctor` in a non-zshrc environment (fresh ssh, bash shell, etc.) can falsely report missing tools that are installed under ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, etc. Make doctor self-contained by prepending common ACFS install dirs to PATH before checks (and optionally improve version detection for commands like go/tmux).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:46:21.82316-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:46:25.893239-05:00"}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lbo","title":"doctor: include user tool dirs in PATH","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh relies on PATH-only lookups, so running `acfs doctor` in a non-zshrc environment (fresh ssh, bash shell, etc.) can falsely report missing tools that are installed under ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, etc. Make doctor self-contained by prepending common ACFS install dirs to PATH before checks (and optionally improve version detection for commands like go/tmux).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:46:21.82316-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","close_reason":"doctor self-contained PATH + version handling"}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mwh","title":"Create VPS provider guides (OVH, Contabo, Hetzner)","description":"# Task: Create VPS Provider Guides

## Description
Create detailed provider-specific guides with screenshots.

## Location
- scripts/providers/ovh.md
- scripts/providers/contabo.md
- scripts/providers/hetzner.md

## Each Guide Contains

### Provider Overview
- Price range for recommended specs
- Pros/cons
- Direct signup link

### Step-by-Step with Screenshots
1. Navigate to VPS section
2. Select Ubuntu 25.x image
3. Choose specs (~$50/mo tier)
4. Add SSH key (screenshot of where)
5. Create server
6. Find IP address (screenshot)

### Provider Quirks
- Default username (root vs ubuntu)
- Firewall setup needed?
- Any DNS/network setup needed?

## Screenshot Placeholders
Use consistent naming:
- ovh-step1-select-os.png
- ovh-step2-add-ssh-key.png
- etc.

## Integration
Website wizard step 5 loads these guides dynamically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Guide for OVH
- [ ] Guide for Contabo
- [ ] Guide for Hetzner
- [ ] Screenshot placeholders
- [ ] Consistent format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:16.271045-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:25:21.350296-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:25:21.350296-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive VPS setup guides for OVH, Contabo, and Hetzner. Each guide includes provider overview, pros/cons, step-by-step instructions, and provider-specific notes. Screenshot placeholders included. Commit 8c725ed.","labels":["documentation","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-my5","title":"acfs update should not abort early when bun is missing","description":"scripts/lib/update.sh runs under set -euo pipefail; update_agents/update_cloud return 1 when bun is missing, which aborts the whole update run and skips unrelated updates (rust/uv/etc). Treat missing bun as a recorded failure (increment FAIL_COUNT) but return 0 so the updater continues and summarizes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:37:57.713727-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:38:42.384269-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:38:42.384269-05:00","close_reason":"Make scripts/lib/update.sh treat missing bun as a recorded failure (FAIL_COUNT) but return 0 so remaining updates continue under set -euo pipefail.","labels":["reliability","tooling"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q10","title":"Harden installer: true dry-run, onboard source, HTTPS PGDG","description":"Fix install.sh --dry-run so it makes no system changes and works without sudo; install onboard command from packages/onboard/onboard.sh (single source of truth); switch PGDG apt repo URL to HTTPS; fix checksum computation in scripts/lib/security.sh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:18:40.054877-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:22:16.261425-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:22:16.261425-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh dry-run to make no system changes + allow running without sudo; install onboard from packages/onboard; switch PGDG repo to HTTPS; fix security.sh checksum logic."}
